recommendations: add a minimal smoke-test suite for all generated MCP endpoints (import + one happy-path call each)
pin and align plugin/runtime dependencies with sktime’s `pyproject.toml` (including optional extras profiles like forecasting/deep-learning)
implement graceful optional-dependency gating per endpoint (clear install hints when `prophet`/`pmdarima`/`torch` etc. are missing)
replace broad base-class endpoints (`BaseForecaster`
`BaseTransformer`
etc.) with task-oriented callable endpoints (fit/predict/evaluate wrappers)
add strict request/response Pydantic schemas for time-series payloads (Series/Panel/Hierarchical) with validation error normalization
introduce endpoint namespacing/versioning (`forecasting.*`
`datasets.*`
`metrics.*`
`v1`) to avoid future breaking changes
expand endpoint coverage to high-value user flows (evaluate/backtesting
model selection pipelines
probabilistic forecasts)
add contract tests to ensure MCP outputs match native sktime API behavior on canonical datasets
cache dataset loader outputs and large model initializations to reduce latency
add structured logging + tracing + per-endpoint timing/exception metrics
generate and publish an endpoint capability matrix from `all_estimators()` tags (supports-multivariate/probabilistic/missing-values)
harden serialization/deserialization for pandas index/fh objects and probabilistic outputs
add security guardrails (input size limits
timeout/cancellation
safe error redaction)
create CI workflow for MCP plugin (lint/type/test/build) independent of core repo CI
add mypy/ruff/pre-commit to `mcp_output` for maintainability
include reproducible example notebooks and curl/JSON examples in `README_MCP.md`
add fallback strategy tests for zip-import path to ensure parity with normal import mode
provide deprecation policy and changelog for endpoint/schema evolution

success_factors: Workflow reached terminal success state with all planned nodes executed (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Import-based adapter strategy was feasible (import feasibility 0.9), enabling direct exposure of sktime APIs.
Generated MCP plugin started successfully and service health was reported as healthy over stdio transport.
Low intrusiveness risk and medium repository complexity were appropriate for automated wrapper generation.
Zip fallback ingestion succeeded on a large codebase (1618 files), preventing pipeline interruption.
failure_reasons: Validation depth is limited: original project tests did not pass/run meaningfully (passed=false, execution_time=0, empty test files).
Quality evidence is incomplete: llm_calls/deepwiki_calls/performance metrics/tool endpoint counts are reported as zero or unknown, reducing confidence in instrumentation and coverage.

node_performance.download_time: Completed successfully; exact per-node timing unavailable. Repo acquisition used zip fallback and handled a large tree, suggesting download/ingestion was robust but likely non-trivial.
node_performance.analysis_time: Completed successfully with broad structural/dependency extraction and core-module identification; likely the dominant cost due to repository size.
node_performance.generation_time: Completed successfully; produced expected MCP scaffold files and endpoint surface. Reported LOC/file-size metrics are zero, indicating telemetry inconsistency rather than no output.
node_performance.test_time: MCP plugin runtime smoke outcome is positive (healthy), but testing rigor is low (no substantive original-project or endpoint contract test execution captured).

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: Unknown from metrics (reported 0 MB). Given repository scale, actual memory use was certainly higher; add real telemetry collection.
resource_usage.cpu_efficiency: Unknown from metrics (reported 0%). CPU profile cannot be assessed reliably without instrumentation.
resource_usage.disk_usage: Moderate-to-high expected due to large source tree and generated artifacts; measured generated size reported as 0 indicates missing/incorrect tracking.
